speaker: PUCELLE
text: Advance our waving colors on the walls.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Rescued is Orleance from the English.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Thus Joan la Pucelle hath performed her word.

speaker: CHARLES
text: Divinest creature, Astraea’s daughter,

speaker: CHARLES
text: How shall I honor thee for this success?

speaker: CHARLES
text: Thy promises are like Adonis’ garden

speaker: CHARLES
text: That one day bloomed and fruitful were the next.

speaker: CHARLES
text: France, triumph in thy glorious prophetess.

speaker: CHARLES
text: Recovered is the town of Orleance.

speaker: CHARLES
text: More blessèd hap did ne’er befall our state.

speaker: REIGNIER
text: Why ring not bells aloud throughout the town?

speaker: REIGNIER
text: Dauphin, command the citizens make bonfires

speaker: REIGNIER
text: And feast and banquet in the open streets

speaker: REIGNIER
text: To celebrate the joy that God hath given us.

speaker: ALANSON
text: All France will be replete with mirth and joy

speaker: ALANSON
text: When they shall hear how we have played the men.

speaker: CHARLES
text: ’Tis Joan, not we, by whom the day is won;

speaker: CHARLES
text: For which I will divide my crown with her,

speaker: CHARLES
text: And all the priests and friars in my realm

speaker: CHARLES
text: Shall in procession sing her endless praise.

speaker: CHARLES
text: A statelier pyramis to her I’ll rear

speaker: CHARLES
text: Than Rhodophe’s of Memphis ever was.

speaker: CHARLES
text: In memory of her, when she is dead,

speaker: CHARLES
text: Her ashes, in an urn more precious

speaker: CHARLES
text: Than the rich-jeweled coffer of Darius,

speaker: CHARLES
text: Transported shall be at high festivals

speaker: CHARLES
text: Before the kings and queens of France.

speaker: CHARLES
text: No longer on Saint Dennis will we cry,

speaker: CHARLES
text: But Joan la Pucelle shall be France’s saint.

speaker: CHARLES
text: Come in, and let us banquet royally

speaker: CHARLES
text: After this golden day of victory.
